title: anil fastigiata
intro: Anil fastigiata, also known as Indian milk bush, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The leaves and roots of Anil fastigiata are used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Anil fastigiata include its ability to reduce fever, treat skin conditions, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues.
